pairs.question: Who was Cecil John Abrahams and which cricket team did he play for in first-class cricket?
pairs.answer: Cecil John Abrahams was a South African cricketer born on 8 March 1932 and died on 15 August 2007. He played briefly in first-class cricket for Western Province in the 1974–75 season during a coaching tour after receiving an invitation from the Western Province Cricket Board.

pairs.question: What were the career highlights of Cecil John Abrahams in English cricket?
pairs.answer: Cecil John Abrahams played in the Central Lancashire League for 15 seasons, starting with Milnrow, followed by Radcliffe and Oldham. His best season was in 1970, where he took 89 wickets at an average of 10.25 runs per wicket, which was the best bowling performance in the league that year by a professional.

pairs.question: How did Cecil John Abrahams contribute to the Lancashire League besides playing?
pairs.answer: Cecil John Abrahams encouraged players from the Western Province Cricket Board to join the Lancashire League, fostering talent and connections between the two cricket communities.

pairs.question: What challenges did Cecil John Abrahams face as a dental technician under apartheid?
pairs.answer: Under apartheid, dental technicians of color were required to work for white dentists. Cecil John Abrahams resisted this by opening his private dental laboratory in Kent Road in Salt River, operating independently to avoid compliance with the discriminatory laws.

pairs.question: Which of Cecil John Abrahams' children had notable cricket achievements?
pairs.answer: His eldest son, John Abrahams, captained the Lancashire county cricket team. His other sons, Peter and Basil, regularly played cricket at weekends.

pairs.question: What inspired Cecil John Abrahams to move to England in 1960?
pairs.answer: In 1960, Cecil John Abrahams received an offer to play cricket in England with Milnrow, arranged by Basil D'Oliviera. This opportunity led to his 15-season career in the Central Lancashire League and eventually brought his family to England in 1962.

pairs.question: Who were the parents of Cecil John Abrahams and what were their contributions to cricket?
pairs.answer: Cecil John Abrahams' father was Isaac John Abrahams, known as the 'Prince of Batsmen' during his time, and his mother was Lillian May (née Schroeder) Abrahams. While his father was noted for his batting prowess, Cecil himself became a prominent cricketer, playing in South Africa and England.
